D3 Chemistry L6 Create Reference

Anti-Stokes Upconversion — Yb³⁺ → Er³⁺ Visible from IR

two low-energy IR photons land on Yb³⁺, energy hops to Er³⁺, which emits one higher-energy visible photon — a single chemistry that defies "energy in, lower energy out"

Lanthanide ions like Yb³⁺ and Er³⁺ have long-lived f-orbital excited states (ms lifetimes). Pump Yb³⁺ at 980 nm IR; energy transfers to Er³⁺; a second IR photon arrives during the long excited-state lifetime; Er³⁺ now has enough energy for a visible green (540 nm) or red (660 nm) emission. Output intensity ∝ I_pump², a tell-tale quadratic signature.

Drag pump-laser intensity. The output green/red emission grows quadratically — every doubling of pump produces 4× output. The log-log plot shows the slope-2 signature.
